channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country’s history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and has since spiraled into wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel defensive briefings surge as adversaries seek to influence congressional races
item.description: Intelligence community officials said they have given three times the number of defensive briefings this year to those who may be the target of foreign influence efforts as in past election cycles.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants in Gaza fire rockets into Israel as it marks Oct. 7
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza fired a barrage of rockets into Israel on Monday as mourners marked the anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, without disrupting a nearby ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former hostage wants ‘peace in the world’ one year later
item.description: One year later, the Chicago-area woman is steadfast in her faith and hopeful others captured that day will be released.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania's opposition protests and demands a caretaker Cabinet
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Opposition supporters in Albania protested again Monday, demanding that the government be replaced by a technocratic caretaker Cabinet before next year’s parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kenya’s deputy president defends himself before impeachment
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s deputy president, facing an impeachment motion in which he's accused of supporting anti-government protests in June and of being involved in corruption and other irregularities, has defended himself and confirmed that he would appear before parliament on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about fighting in Lebanon and Gaza
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza and Lebanon fired waves of rockets into Israel as the nation mourned the anniversary of the Oct. 7 Hamas attack that killed 1,200 people and set off a war that has engulfed Gaza and parts of Lebanon and threatened to ignite a wider regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Belarusian-American has his prison sentence extended amid continuous crackdown on dissent in Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — A Belarusian-American has had his prison term extended to a total of 13 1/2 years in the latest move in a relentless crackdown on dissent by Belarus' repressive government, rights activists said Monday. Yuras Zyankovich, a lawyer who has dual Belarusian and U.S.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Harris, Emhoff mark anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff on Monday marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks by Hamas on Israel. The two were expected to plant a memorial tree at the vice president’s residence, in addition to delivering remarks to the press.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Thousands survived a brutal gang attack in Haiti that killed 70. Now they face an uncertain future
item.description: Thousands of Haitians are facing an uncertain future after surviving a brutal gang attack last week in a small town that killed at least 70 people.
item.dc:creator: Pierre-Richard Luxama And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A Pakistani separatist group claims bombing that killed 2 Chinese near Karachi airport
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A Pakistani separatist group claimed responsibility for a late-night bombing that targeted a convoy with Chinese nationals outside the country's largest airport, killing two workers from China and wounding eight people, officials and the insurgent group said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's North Darfur kills at least 13 children, UNICEF says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Fighting between the Sudanese military and its rival paramilitary in Sudan ’s North Darfur killed at least 13 children and injured four others, UNICEF said. The children were between 6 and 17 years old, the U.N. agency said in a statement on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BY FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sao Paulo mayor's race heads to a runoff without a controversial outsider in Brazil's elections
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Incumbent Ricardo Nunes and leftist lawmaker Guilherme Boulos will face each other in a runoff to decide the next mayor of Sao Paulo, following a first round of voting Sunday in Brazil’s local elections.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden orders 500 more active-duty troops to North Carolina to aid in Helene recovery
item.description: President Biden ordered the Department of Defense to move an additional 500 active-duty troops into western North Carolina to aid in the recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene, the White House announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Iran lifts flight restrictions ahead of schedule after grounding all flights for 'operational restrictions'
item.description: Flight restrictions were lifted in Iran after safe conditions were confirmed, state media reported on Monday, shortening the period of flight cancellations announced earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia strikes Ukraine with drones and missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces attacked Ukraine overnight with 87 Shahed drones and four different types of missiles, officials said Sunday. A 49-year-old man was killed in the Kharkiv region after his car was hit by a drone, said regional Gov. Oleh Syniehubov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope names 21 new cardinals, significantly increasing the pool who will one day elect his successor
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis named 21 new cardinals Sunday, significantly increasing the size of the College of Cardinals and further cementing his mark on the group of prelates who will one day elect his successor.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military kills 250 Hezbollah terrorists since start of limited ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military says it has killed 250 Hezbollah terrorists and has struck more than 2,000 military targets since it launched a limited ground operation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: Officials said an investigation by the Self-Defense Forces and police confirmed that the explosion was caused by a 500-pound U.S. bomb and there was no further danger.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran says it fired dozens of missiles into Israel, where residents are warned to shelter in place
item.description: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel. Israel warned Lebanese people earlier Tuesday to evacuate border communities to avoid harm from limited ground operations it said were launched against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation
